relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul names the spell: crucified Messiah has been displaced before their eyes.
The Spirit came through hearing trust, not boundary-works.
What began in Spirit cannot be completed by flesh-pattern.
Their sufferings must not become empty.
Spirit and powers among them came through trust-hearing.
Abraham trusted, and it was counted as justice.
Those from trust are Abraham’s children.
Scripture foresaw nations made just through trust and announced blessing to Abraham.
Trusting ones share blessing with trusting Abraham.
Boundary-works as a righteousness-regime place one under curse.
The righteous one lives from trust.
Law and trust operate differently in Paul’s argument.
Messiah enters the curse-place to release us from curse.
Abraham’s blessing reaches the nations, and Spirit-promise is received through trust.
A confirmed covenant is not simply set aside.
Promise narrows into the seed, Messiah.
The later Law cannot make the earlier promise inactive.
Inheritance comes by promise-grace, not Law-possession.
Law was added until the promised seed came.
Mediator language yields to the oneness of the Divine.
Law is not against promise; it simply cannot make alive.
Scripture encloses all under misalignment so promise may be given through Messiah’s trust/faithfulness.
Before trust’s unveiling, Law guarded the people.
Law served as guardian until Messiah.
Trust has come; guardian-regime is no longer the container.
All are Divine children through trust in Messiah.
Baptized into Messiah, they are clothed with Messiah.
Judean/Greek, enslaved/free, male/female no longer define access to oneness in Messiah.
Belonging to Messiah makes them Abraham’s seed and promise-heirs.

contemplative_note: Galatians 3 is promise before possession. Spirit, trust, and Messiah-clothing open one family where old access markers cannot rule the table.
